findings.evidence: `CircleFundamentalGroup.lean:70` proves `Deck.isRegular_addCircleCoe` for arbitrary `𝕜`; `UniversalCover/AddCircle.lean:158` has `Deck.addCircleMulEquivInt` for arbitrary preconnected `𝕜` with totally disconnected `zmultiples p`; `Deck/FundamentalGroup.lean:72` has the generic `IsRegular.fundamentalGroupEquiv`.
findings.fix: First state a generic theorem for `{𝕜} [AddCommGroup 𝕜] [TopologicalSpace 𝕜] [IsTopologicalAddGroup 𝕜] [SimplyConnectedSpace 𝕜] [PreconnectedSpace 𝕜] [TotallyDisconnectedSpace (zmultiples p)]`, assuming `IsCoveringMap ((↑) : 𝕜 → AddCircle p)` and `¬ IsOfFinAddOrder p`; then derive the real `p ≠ 0` version from it.
findings.issue: `AddCircle.fundamentalGroupMulEquiv` hard-codes `p : ℝ` and `hp : p ≠ 0`, but the proof only uses generic regularity, a covering map, simple connectedness of the total space, and the generic cyclic deck-group computation.

summary: The main equivalence is stated too specifically for the real circle even though the surrounding API already supports the same argument for any suitable additive quotient cover.
